items.prompt: What is the primary characteristic synthetic data must preserve?
choices: Exact copies of individual real records
Analytical value through similar statistical structure and patterns
Complete absence of any algorithmic generation
Higher noise than the original dataset in every field
items.explain: Section 10.1.1 stresses that synthetic data should resemble original data in ways that preserve analytical value.

items.prompt: Example 10.1 shows that fidelity requirements are the same for synthetic images and synthetic financial transactions.
items.explain: Example 10.1 contrasts modality-specific fidelity: realistic visual patterns versus preserved transaction statistics.

items.prompt: Which benefit does Example 10.2 primarily illustrate?
items.explain: Example 10.2 describes synthetic patient records that mimic real medical data without exposing actual patients.

items.prompt: Name two benefits of synthetic data besides privacy.
items.rubric: Mention cost efficiency and/or scalability (or rare-scenario coverage).
items.sample_answer: Synthetic data reduces collection cost and can be generated at scale to cover rare or underrepresented scenarios.

items.prompt: Synthetic driving scenarios (Example 10.3) help address costly and hard-to-capture real-world conditions.
items.explain: Example 10.3 notes simulations for weather, lighting, and road environments that are difficult to capture in real life.
